--- title: "Hands-on_Ex0301" author: "JUNCHEN LI" date: "april 30,2026" date-modified: "last-modified" format: html editor: visual execute: eval: true echo: true warning: false freeze: auto --- ::: panel-tabset ## 3.2 Getting Started ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} pacman::p_load(ggiraph, plotly, patchwork, DT, tidyverse) ``` ::: ## 3.3 Importing the data ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv") ``` ::: ::: ## 3.4.1 Tooltip effect with tooltip aesthetic ::: panel-tabset ## Graph Ex: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code Ex: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(tooltip = ID), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618 ) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Changing variable(self) plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = ENGLISH)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(tooltip = RACE), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618 ) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.5.1 Displaying multiple information on tooltip ::: panel-tabset ## plot Ex ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam_data$tooltip <- c(paste0( "Name = ", exam_data$ID, " Class = ", exam_data$CLASS)) p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(tooltip = exam_data$tooltip), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 8, height_svg = 8*0.618 ) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ### Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam_data$tooltip <- c(paste0( "GENDER = ", exam_data$GENDER, " RACE = ", exam_data$RACE)) p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = SCIENCE)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(tooltip = exam_data$tooltip), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 8, height_svg = 8*0.618 ) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.6.1 Customising Tooltip style ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} tooltip_css <- "background-color:white; #<< font-style:bold; color:black;" #<< p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(tooltip = ID), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618, options = list( #<< opts_tooltip( #<< css = tooltip_css)) #<< ) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Tyring plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} tooltip_css <- "background-color:Red; #<< font-style:bold; color:Yellow;" #<< p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = ENGLISH)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(tooltip = GENDER), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618, options = list( #<< opts_tooltip( #<< css = tooltip_css)) #<< ) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.6.2 Displaying statistics on tooltip ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} tooltip <- function(y, ymax, accuracy = .01) { mean <- scales::number(y, accuracy = accuracy) sem <- scales::number(ymax - y, accuracy = accuracy) paste("Mean maths scores:", mean, "+/-", sem) } gg_point <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = RACE), ) + stat_summary(aes(y = MATHS, tooltip = after_stat( tooltip(y, ymax))), fun.data = "mean_se", geom = GeomInteractiveCol, fill = "light blue" ) + stat_summary(aes(y = MATHS), fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2, size = 0.2 ) girafe(ggobj = gg_point, width_svg = 8, height_svg = 8*0.618) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} tooltip <- function(y, ymax, accuracy = .001) { med <- scales::number(y, accuracy = accuracy) sem <- scales::number(ymax - y, accuracy = accuracy) paste("Median SCIENCE scores:", med, "+/-", sem) } median_sd <- function(x) { m <- median(x, na.rm = TRUE) s <- sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) data.frame(y = m, ymin = m - s, ymax = m + s) } gg_point <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = GENDER), ) + stat_summary(aes(y = SCIENCE, tooltip = after_stat( tooltip(y, ymax))), fun.data = median_sd, geom = GeomInteractiveCol, fill = "light blue" ) + stat_summary(aes(y = SCIENCE), fun.data = median_sd, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2, size = 0.2 ) girafe(ggobj = gg_point, width_svg = 8, height_svg = 8*0.618) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.6.3 Hover effect with data_id aesthetic ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(data_id = CLASS), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618 ) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p <- ggplot(data = exam_data, aes(x = ENGLISH)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(data_id = CLASS), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "dotdensity" ) + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618 ) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.6.4 Styling hover effect ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(data_id = CLASS), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618, options = list( opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"), opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;") ) ) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = ENGLISH)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(data_id = CLASS), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "dotdensity") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618, options = list( opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"), opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;") ) ) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.6.5 Combining tooltip and hover effect ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(tooltip = CLASS, data_id = CLASS), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618, options = list( opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"), opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;") ) ) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = SCIENCE)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(tooltip = RACE, data_id = RACE), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "dotdensity") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618, options = list( opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"), opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;") ) ) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.6.6 Click effect with onclick ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam_data$onclick <- sprintf("window.open("%s%s")", "https://www.moe.gov.sg/schoolfinder?journey=Primary%20school", as.character(exam_data$ID)) p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(onclick = onclick), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} exam_data$onclick <- sprintf("window.open("%s%s")", "https://www.moe.gov.sg/schoolfinder?journey=Primary%20school", as.character(exam_data$ID)) p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = ENGLISH)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(onclick = onclick), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "dotdensity") + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe( ggobj = p, width_svg = 6, height_svg = 6*0.618) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.6.7 Coordinated Multiple Views with ggiraph ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p1 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(data_id = ID), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100)) + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) p2 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = ENGLISH)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(data_id = ID), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100)) + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe(code = print(p1 + p2), width_svg = 6, height_svg = 3, options = list( opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"), opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;") ) ) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p1 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(data_id = GENDER), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100)) + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) p2 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = SCIENCE)) + geom_dotplot_interactive( aes(data_id = GENDER), stackgroups = TRUE, binwidth = 1, method = "histodot") + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100)) + scale_y_continuous(NULL, breaks = NULL) girafe(code = print(p1 + p2), width_svg = 6, height_svg = 3, options = list( opts_hover(css = "fill: #202020;"), opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.2;") ) ) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.7.1 Creating an interactive scatter plot: plot_ly() method ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_ly(data = exam_data, x = ~MATHS, y = ~ENGLISH) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_ly(data = exam_data, x = ~SCIENCE, y = ~ENGLISH) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.7.2 Working with visual variable: plot_ly() method ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_ly(data = exam_data, x = ~ENGLISH, y = ~MATHS, color = ~RACE) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plot_ly(data = exam_data, x = ~SCIENCE, y = ~MATHS, color = ~CLASS) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.7.3 Creating an interactive scatter plot: ggplotly() method ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) + geom_point(size=1) + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100), ylim=c(0,100)) ggplotly(p) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} p <- ggplot(data=exam_data, aes(x = SCIENCE, y = ENGLISH)) + geom_point(size=1) + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100), ylim=c(0,100)) ggplotly(p) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.7.4 Coordinated Multiple Views with plotly ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} d <- highlight_key(exam_data) p1 <- ggplot(data=d, aes(x = MATHS, y = ENGLISH)) + geom_point(size=1) + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100), ylim=c(0,100)) p2 <- ggplot(data=d, aes(x = MATHS, y = SCIENCE)) + geom_point(size=1) + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100), ylim=c(0,100)) subplot(ggplotly(p1), ggplotly(p2)) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} d <- highlight_key(exam_data) p1 <- ggplot(data=d, aes(x = SCIENCE, y = ENGLISH)) + geom_point(size=1) + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100), ylim=c(0,100)) p2 <- ggplot(data=d, aes(x = MATHS, y = SCIENCE)) + geom_point(size=1) + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100), ylim=c(0,100)) subplot(ggplotly(p1), ggplotly(p2)) ``` ::: ::: ## 3.8.1 Interactive Data Table: DT package ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} DT::datatable(exam_data, class= "compact") ``` ::: ::: ## 3.8.2 Linked brushing: crosstalk method ::: panel-tabset ## Ex plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} d <- highlight_key(exam_data) p <- ggplot(d, aes(ENGLISH, MATHS)) + geom_point(size=1) + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100), ylim=c(0,100)) gg <- highlight(ggplotly(p), "plotly_selected") crosstalk::bscols(gg, DT::datatable(d), widths = 5) ``` ::: ::: ::: panel-tabset ## Self Trying plot ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Code chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} d <- highlight_key(exam_data) p <- ggplot(d, aes(SCIENCE, MATHS)) + geom_point(size=1) + coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100), ylim=c(0,100)) gg <- highlight(ggplotly(p), "plotly_selected") crosstalk::bscols(gg, DT::datatable(d), widths = 5) ``` ::: :::
